title: Independent Sales Consultant
setting: Self-employed sales consultant or agent working with multiple clients across industries
companySize: 1 person (you) with a network of clients
description: You're an independent sales consultant — you either represent multiple products/services as a commission-based agent, or you consult with companies to build and fix their sales processes. You set your own hours, choose your clients, and your income is entirely performance-based. It's the ultimate test of sales skill: no salary safety net, no company brand behind you, just your ability to create value and close deals.
salary: $100,000-$250,000+ AUD variable. No base salary — income is 100% commission, retainer, or project-based. Top independent consultants earning $300K-$500K+ are not uncommon. Income is volatile: some months are $30K+, some months are lean. Financial discipline is essential.

schedule.activity: Morning strategy: review the day's plan, prioritise client work, check overnight messages from international clients
schedule.note: As an independent, you're the CEO of your own business. Strategy time is non-negotiable.

schedule.activity: Content and brand building: post on LinkedIn, write a short article or comment on industry content to build your personal brand
schedule.note: Your personal brand IS your lead generation engine. No company marketing team is doing this for you.

schedule.activity: Client A work: discovery call for a SaaS client you represent — selling their analytics platform to a mid-market retailer
schedule.note: As an agent, you represent the product but bring your own relationships and credibility.

schedule.activity: Client B consulting: review the pipeline of a startup you're advising. Coach their two AEs on deal strategy and discovery skills.
schedule.note: Consulting clients pay retainers ($5K-$15K/month) for your expertise in building their sales function.

schedule.activity: Proposal writing: draft a consulting proposal for a new prospective client - a B2B SaaS company that needs help fixing pipeline discipline and outbound execution
schedule.note: You're selling yourself. Your proposal needs to demonstrate deep expertise and quantify the ROI of your services.

schedule.activity: Business development: reach out to 5 potential consulting clients. Follow up on 3 warm introductions from your referral network.
schedule.note: Independent consultants live and die by their network. Referrals are your highest-converting lead source.

schedule.activity: Networking lunch: meet a former colleague who's now a VP Sales at a growing company — potential client or referral partner
schedule.note: Build relationships before you need them. Your network is your pipeline.

schedule.activity: Client A work: follow up on yesterday's demo with a personalised Loom video and proposal for the retailer deal
schedule.note: Speed and quality of follow-up is where independents differentiate. No bureaucracy, no approval chains — you move fast.

schedule.activity: Client C training: run a 90-minute sales training workshop for a tech company's SDR team on cold calling and discovery
schedule.note: Training workshops are a high-value, time-efficient revenue stream. $2K-$5K per session.

schedule.activity: Admin: invoicing, expense tracking, updating your CRM with all client activities, reviewing monthly financials
schedule.note: Nobody does your admin for you. Set aside time weekly or it piles up.

schedule.activity: Learning and skill development: read industry reports, take an online course, or review the latest sales methodology research
schedule.note: Your expertise is your product. If you stop learning, your value declines.

schedule.activity: Planning: review the week, assess client pipeline, plan next week's priorities, update financial projections
schedule.note: Weekly planning prevents the feast-or-famine cycle that kills independent consultants.

responsibilities: Generate your own pipeline through networking, referrals, content marketing, and outbound
Manage multiple client relationships simultaneously with different products, cycles, and expectations
Deliver sales consulting: pipeline reviews, coaching, training, and process design
Represent client products as a commission-based agent — running the full sales cycle
Build and maintain your personal brand as a trusted sales expert
Handle all business operations: invoicing, tax, insurance, contracts, and financial planning
Continuously develop your expertise to justify premium consulting rates
Maintain a referral network that generates consistent warm introductions
challenges: No base salary — income is 100% dependent on your ability to generate business
Feast-or-famine cycles: some months are excellent, others are quiet
Wearing every hat: sales, marketing, operations, finance, and administration
No team support: no SDR booking your meetings, no SE supporting your demos, no manager coaching you
Client management: balancing competing priorities across multiple clients
Loneliness: working independently without a team for daily motivation and support
Tax complexity: managing GST, BAS statements, and quarterly tax obligations as a sole trader or company
rewards: Complete autonomy: you choose your clients, your hours, and your focus
Uncapped income: no corporate salary bands or quota caps. You earn what you're worth.
Variety: every day is different. Multiple clients, industries, and challenges keep the work stimulating.
Building equity: your reputation, network, and client relationships are assets that compound over time
Location independence: most work can be done remotely from anywhere
The satisfaction of building something that's entirely yours
Premium rates: experienced independent sales consultants charge $200-$400+ per hour
Work-life flexibility: you design your schedule around your life, not the other way around
careerPath: SDR/BDR at a tech company (0-1 year) — learn the fundamentals of prospecting and qualification
Account Executive (1-5 years) — master full-cycle selling and build your network
Senior AE or Sales Manager (5-8 years) — develop expertise and industry reputation
Independent Sales Consultant (8+ years) — leverage your network and expertise for premium clients
Boutique Sales Agency (10+ years) — hire other consultants and scale beyond your own time
Fractional CRO/VP Sales (12+ years) — serve as a part-time sales leader for multiple growth-stage companies
Advisory Board Member (15+ years) — leverage your network and expertise for equity-based advisory roles
